title: Puerto Banus Dolphin Watching Boat Hire 2026: Guide and Costs
html:
Puerto Banus offers exceptional dolphin watching boat hire from its marina, with half-day private charters starting around EUR 650 for a family-friendly trip into the Bay of Marbella. BoatHire24 connects you with verified operators who know where the striped dolphins and pilot whales surface, making an afternoon on the Mediterranean unforgettable.

How much does it cost to rent a boat for dolphin watching in Puerto Banus?

Prices range from EUR 500 for a shared tour to over EUR 4,000 for a crewed superyacht, depending on boat size and duration. A small motorboat for half a day typically costs EUR 650, 1,200, a catamaran full-day around EUR 2,000, 3,000, and a crewed yacht with skipper EUR 1,500, 4,000. You can book a charter online and compare prices instantly.

Where to set off in Puerto Banus

The main departure point is Puerto Banus marina itself, where dozens of charter boats line the dock. Nearby alternatives include Puerto José Banus (the older section) and the launch ramp at San Pedro de Alcántara. For a longer sail, set off from Marbella's Real Club Náutico or Cabopino harbour, both within 10 nautical miles. The best dolphin grounds lie 3, 5 miles off the coast between Punta de Calaburras and the Cabo de San Roque lighthouse.

Which boat to rent

  1. Motor yacht (e.g. Sunseeker Predator 68), best for speed and dolphin chasing, up to 12 guests, from EUR 1,200 half-day.
  2. Catamaran (e.g. Lagoon 52), stable and spacious for families, up to 10 guests, from EUR 2,000 full-day.
  3. Rib or Axopar 28, nimble for small groups of 6, from EUR 650 half-day.
  4. Crewed superyacht (e.g. Azimut 80), luxury experience for 8, from EUR 3,500 full-day.

For a reliable family option, consider the Mefasa 90 for hire, a 28-metre motor yacht with spacious decks and an onboard sound system.

Costs at a glance

  • Price band: EUR 500, 4,000 per trip
  • Peak months: June to August (also warm in May and September)
  • Trip length: 3, 8 hours
  • Capacity: 6, 12 guests typical
  • Currency: EUR

Do you need a licence to rent a boat for dolphin watching in Puerto Banus?

No licence is required for most rental boats as they come with a qualified skipper. In Spain, private boat operators must hold a recreational licence for boat types over a certain power, but charter companies always include a professional captain. The operator will confirm exact requirements at booking.

Local tip: morning trips between 9am and 12pm offer the best dolphin sightings, as the sea is usually calm and the animals feed closer to the surface. Ask your skipper to head toward the Banus seamount, a known hotspot.

When is the best season for dolphin watching in Puerto Banus?

High season runs from June through September, with July and August offering the warmest water and most consistent sightings. The prevailing Levante wind can make the sea choppy in August afternoons, so morning trips are recommended. Spring and autumn are quieter and often cheaper.

Questions about boat rental in Puerto Banus

Can you see dolphins from Puerto Banus year-round?

Yes, striped dolphins and common dolphins are present in the Alboran Sea all year, though sightings are most reliable from April to October.

What happens if the weather is bad on the day of my booking?

Most operators offer a full refund or rescheduling if rough weather forces cancellation, as safety is paramount.

How far do you go to find dolphins?

Typical trips cover 5, 12 nautical miles offshore, reaching depths where dolphin pods forage.

Can I bring my own food and drink on board?

Yes, most charter boats allow you to bring your own provisions, and many have a galley or cooler for storage.

To find the perfect vessel, use the boat search to filter by size, amenities, and price. BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Puerto Banus or list your own. For owners, you can check availability and start earning from your boat immediately. In 2026, demand for dolphin watching charters in Puerto Banus continues to grow, making it a smart season to list.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Puerto Banus and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
